company.name: ExecutiveAI Demo Company
company.description: A fictional SaaS company providing subscription-based software to individual and enterprise customers.

USERS.purpose: Stores one record for every registered customer.

USER_ID.description: Unique identifier for each registered customer.

DEVICE.description: Primary device used by the customer.

PAID.description: Indicates whether the customer is a paid subscriber.

business_questions: How many users do we have?
How many paid users?
Users by subscription plan
Users by country
Top customers by lifetime value
Customers with low health score

SESSIONS.purpose: Stores every application session initiated by a user.

SESSION_ID.description: Unique identifier for each application session.

PAGES.description: Number of pages or screens viewed during the session.

CRASHED.description: Indicates whether the application crashed during the session.

business_questions: How many sessions occurred today?
What is the average session duration?
Which users have the highest number of sessions?
What is the application crash rate?
How many pages do users view per session?
What are the Daily Active Users (DAU)?
What are the Weekly Active Users (WAU)?
What are the Monthly Active Users (MAU)?

FEATURE_USAGE.purpose: Tracks every feature used by a customer during an application session.
FEATURE_USAGE.granularity: One row per feature usage event

USAGE_ID.description: Unique identifier for the feature usage event.

USER_ID.description: Reference to the customer who used the feature.

FEATURE.description: Name of the feature used by the customer.

USES.description: Number of times the feature was used during the session.

AI_TOKENS.description: Total AI tokens consumed by this feature usage.

business_questions: Which feature is used the most?
How many times was AI Chat used?
Which users consume the most AI tokens?
What is the total AI token usage?
Which features have the highest adoption?
Which session generated the highest feature usage?
Which customers are the most active feature users?
What are the top 10 most frequently used features?

SUBSCRIPTIONS.purpose: Stores every subscription lifecycle event including new subscriptions, upgrades, downgrades, renewals, cancellations, and billing.

SUBSCRIPTION_ID.description: Unique identifier for the subscription event.

EVENT_DATE.description: Date when the subscription event occurred.

EVENT.description: Type of subscription event such as New, Upgrade, Downgrade, Renewal or Cancel.

AMOUNT.description: Revenue amount associated with the subscription event.

business_questions: What is our Monthly Recurring Revenue?
What is our Annual Recurring Revenue?
How much revenue did each plan generate?
How many upgrades occurred this month?
How many downgrades occurred this month?
How many cancellations occurred?
Which subscription plan is the most popular?
What is the monthly subscription growth?
What is the average subscription value?
Who are the highest paying customers?

MARKETING_CAMPAIGNS.purpose: Stores marketing campaign performance metrics used to measure customer acquisition, conversion, and advertising effectiveness.
MARKETING_CAMPAIGNS.granularity: One row per campaign per reporting month

CAMPAIGN_ID.description: Unique identifier for the marketing campaign.

BUDGET.description: Marketing budget allocated to the campaign.

INSTALLS.description: Total application installs generated by the campaign.

CUSTOMERS.description: Number of customers acquired through the campaign.

ROAS.description: Return On Ad Spend for the campaign.

business_questions: Which campaign generated the highest ROAS?
Which marketing channel acquired the most customers?
How much marketing budget was spent this month?
Which campaign generated the most installs?
Which campaign has the highest conversion rate?
What is the Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC)?
Which channel delivers the best ROI?
How many customers were acquired through each campaign?
Show monthly marketing performance.
Compare campaign performance by channel.

ENTERPRISE_ACCOUNTS.purpose: Stores information about enterprise customers including company profile, industry, purchased licenses, and overall account health.

ACCOUNT_ID.description: Unique identifier for the enterprise account.

INDUSTRY.description: Industry to which the customer belongs.

HEALTH.description: Overall customer health status such as Healthy, At Risk, or Critical.

business_questions: How many enterprise customers do we have?
Which industry has the highest number of enterprise customers?
Which enterprise customers purchased the most seats?
How many licenses have been sold?
Which customers are marked as At Risk?
Show enterprise customers by industry.
What is the average number of seats per customer?
Which enterprise customer has the largest deployment?
How many Healthy vs At Risk customers do we have?
List all enterprise customers.

SALES_PIPELINE.purpose: Stores enterprise sales opportunities from lead creation to deal closure, including deal stage, owner, and contract value.

DEAL_ID.description: Unique identifier for the sales opportunity.

STAGE.description: Current stage of the sales opportunity such as Prospecting, Qualification, Proposal, Negotiation, Closed Won, or Closed Lost.

OWNER.description: Sales representative responsible for the opportunity.

ACV.description: Annual Contract Value of the sales opportunity.

business_questions: What is the total sales pipeline value?
What is the pipeline value by stage?
Which sales representative owns the largest pipeline?
What is the average Annual Contract Value?
How many deals are currently in negotiation?
Which deals have the highest contract value?
Show pipeline by sales representative.
Show pipeline by enterprise customer.
Which sales stage has the highest number of opportunities?
What is the expected annual revenue from the pipeline?

SUPPORT_TICKETS.purpose: Stores customer support requests, issue categories, priorities, resolution metrics, and customer satisfaction scores.

TICKET_ID.description: Unique identifier for each support ticket.

USER_ID.description: Reference to the customer who raised the ticket.

OPENED.description: Date when the support ticket was created.

CATEGORY.description: Issue category such as Billing, Login, Performance, AI, or Bug.

PRIORITY.description: Business priority of the ticket such as Low, Medium, High, or Critical.

LINKED_RELEASE.description: Product release or version associated with the reported issue.

RESOLUTION_HOURS.description: Total time taken to resolve the support ticket in hours.

CSAT.description: Customer Satisfaction score provided after ticket resolution.

business_questions: How many support tickets were created this month?
What is the average resolution time?
What is the average CSAT score?
Which issue category has the most tickets?
How many high priority tickets are currently open?
Which product release generated the most support tickets?
Show tickets by priority.
Show tickets by category.
Which customers raised the most support tickets?
What is the customer satisfaction trend over time?

TICKET_ID.description: Unique identifier for each support ticket.

AI_REQUESTS.purpose: Stores every AI request made by customers, including the model used, prompt category, token consumption, and estimated processing cost.

REQUEST_ID.description: Unique identifier for the AI request.

USER_ID.description: Reference to the customer making the AI request.

REQUEST_DATE.description: Date when the AI request was submitted.

MODEL.description: AI model used to process the request.

PROMPT_TYPE.description: Category of prompt such as Chat, Summarization, Translation, Code Generation, or Analytics.

TOKENS.description: Total tokens consumed by the request.

COST_USD.description: Estimated processing cost of the AI request in USD.

business_questions: How many AI requests were processed?
What is the total AI processing cost?
How many AI tokens were consumed?
Which AI model is used the most?
Which prompt type is most common?
Which customers consume the most AI tokens?
Show AI cost by month.
Show token consumption by model.
Which prompt category generates the highest cost?
What is the average cost per AI request?

FINANCE_TRANSACTIONS.purpose: Stores all financial transactions including revenue and expenses used for financial reporting and profitability analysis.

TXN_ID.description: Unique identifier for the financial transaction.

TYPE.description: Transaction type such as Revenue or Expense.

CATEGORY.description: Financial category such as Subscription, Enterprise Sales, Marketing, Infrastructure, Support, or AI.

business_questions: What is the total revenue?
What are the total expenses?
What is the monthly revenue trend?
Show revenue by category.
Show expenses by category.
Which category generates the highest revenue?
What is the total AI spending?
Compare revenue versus expenses.
Show financial transactions by month.
What is the average transaction value?

PRODUCT_RELEASES.purpose: Stores product release history including software versions, release dates, major features, and known issues. This table helps correlate customer support trends with product releases.

RELEASE_DATE.description: Date when the product version was released.

FEATURE.description: Major feature or enhancement introduced in the release.

ISSUE.description: Known issue associated with the release. 'None' indicates no known issues.

business_questions: What is the latest product release?
Which features were released this month?
Show the product release timeline.
Which release introduced AI Dashboard?
Which release generated the most support tickets?
Show releases with known issues.
How many releases were deployed this year?
What features were introduced in each version?
Which release had the highest customer impact?
List all product versions.
